question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail whether there is any projected underspend within her Department.
answer_text: My Department carefully managed 2023-24 spend against budget, with minimal underspend forecast. The outturn position is currently being audited.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what steps she has taken to increase the number of social homes delivered each year.
answer_text: NIHE has set out its Programme of Works to expand the SHDP which was signed off by the Department in October 2022.  My officials and NIHE are currently taking these actions forward.One of the critical issues is the provision of suitable land in areas of housing need.  My Department has recently approved a Housing Executive Land Acquisition business case for the identification and acquisition of land in public ownership to support the continued development of social housing through the Social Housing Development Programme.  My officials are also looking at ways in which we can make delivery timelier.  My ability to increase the supply of social housing is constrained by budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the Northern Ireland Housing Executive maintenance schemes which are impacted by the ongoing contractor dispute.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for The Northern Ireland Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:     “In October 2022 the Housing Executives Board took the decision to release 10 contractors from its CT055 planned maintenance contracts due to the significant inflationary increases in cost being experienced at the time. The Housing Executive has procured replacement contracts which commenced in September 2023 and are currently delivering works”. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on a fuel poverty strategy.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer I gave to AQW 6599/22-27

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Anti-Poverty Strategy.
answer_text: Addressing poverty in all its forms is a key priority for me and I am committed to delivering long-term, sustainable solutions to poverty for our communities right across Northern Ireland.	I am currently considering the next steps to be taken in relation to the Executive Anti-Poverty Strategy. Final decisions on the content and implementation of the Anti-Poverty Strategy will be subject to Executive agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the total number of blue badge applications currently pending.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the current clearance time for blue badge applications.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 4709/22-27, to detail the average time taken to process a Disabled Persons’ Allowance application.
answer_text: Processing times for Disabled Person’s Allowance can differ significantly depending on a wide range of factors specific to each individual application.Applicants may be required to provide additional information, supporting evidence or agree to an inspection of their property.  In cases like this, it can take time for the applicant to obtain and provide the necessary information, particularly when that information needs to be sourced from third parties.  By comparison, where no further information is required, applications can be assessed and processed on the day they are received.  Based on cases processed in the current rating year, the average time taken is 41 days.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the (i) location; and (ii) status of the vacant Education Authority sites in East Belfast.
answer_text: The Education Authority currently has two vacant sites within the East Belfast constituency.PropertyAddressCurrent StatusOrangefield High School (land only)Cameronian Drive, Belfast, BT5 6AWRetained for future educational useTullycarnet Primary SchoolKings Road, Belfast, BT5 7EHRetained for future educational use

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 4709/22-27, to detail the number of households waiting for a decision on Disabled Persons’ Allowance applications as of 20 October 2022.
answer_text: As of 20th October 2022, there were 411 households waiting for a decision on Disabled Person’s Allowance.The majority of these applications have been received very recently, although some have been awaiting a decision for a longer period of time.  Land & Property Services is taking proactive steps to process all applications in a timely manner.  Where applications meet eligibility criteria and have sufficient supporting information these are processed quickly.  Some applications require additional information or evidence and it can take time for the applicant to obtain and provide the necessary information, particularly when that information needs to be sourced from third parties. Due to the nature of the relief, property inspections for DPA had been postponed during the Pandemic due to the potential vulnerable nature of the household.  These applications are now being revisited and LPS staff will be agreeing suitable visiting times with ratepayers to enable their application to progress.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the measures under consideration by her Department to support carers impacted by the cost of living crisis.
answer_text: My officials have worked collaboratively across departments to provide information on all support available in relation to the cost of living crisis. This information is available on NI Direct’s Cost of Living webpage: https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/cost-living.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 3868/22-27, to provide a breakdown detailing the business area to which each letter relates.
answer_text: Whistleblower concerns frequently relate to the business area in which an individual is employed and so this information is treated in confidence.  As the number of concerns in each year is small, further breakdown may result in a risk that individuals who have raised concerns may become identifiable.  A breakdown is not therefore provided.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of free school meal applications which are yet to be processed.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is responsible for the processing of applications for free school meals. There are currently no outstanding applications as due to the small numbers received at this time of year, applications are processed each day.The EA strives for continuous improvement across all of its services and made changes for the current academic year which have resulted in improved messages and guidance to parents in relation to free school meal applications. These changes have enabled a faster processing time for applications resulting in children being able to access free school meals on a more timely basis.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the steps her Department is taking to ensure each child who is entitled receives free school meals.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is responsible for the processing of applications for free school meals. There are currently no outstanding applications as due to the small numbers received at this time of year, applications are processed each day.The EA strives for continuous improvement across all of its services and made changes for the current academic year which have resulted in improved messages and guidance to parents in relation to free school meal applications. These changes have enabled a faster processing time for applications resulting in children being able to access free school meals on a more timely basis.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail all pilot Northern Ireland Housing Executive projects delivered in East Belfast, in each the last five years, broken down by location and project type.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“A Social Prescribing model was piloted in Belfast (Jan 2023 - Dec 2023). The project, called Support Connect, was funded through the Housing Executive Supporting People Provider Innovation fund and was a collaboration between Clanmil Housing Association and Alpha Housing. This pilot was aimed to promote the inclusion and wellbeing of older people, bolstering two hubs (Clanmil HA - Hughes Court, BT6 in East Belfast and Alpha Housing - Carrs Court, BT12 in South Belfast), to extend floating housing support services and leverage existing community-based services. This was aimed at improving outcomes for existing tenants and older people living within a two-mile radius of each of the hubs in the local community.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 1083/22-27, whether the terms of reference for the reconvened Emergencies Leadership Group have been drafted.
answer_text: Former Minister Hargey reconvened the VCS Emergencies Leadership Group in response to the Cost-of-Living crisis in June 2022. A Terms of Reference was drafted and agreed by members of the Group. I have asked my officials to share this with the Member.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission for an update on the rollout of automated doors on the 2nd, 3rd and 4th floors of Parliament Buildings.
answer_text: As noted in the replies to AQW 773/22-28 and AQW 1005/22-27, the Assembly Commission is acutely aware of the need to ensure that Parliament Buildings is accessible for everyone.While there are currently no plans to extend door automation beyond the existing three floors (i.e. the Basement, Ground and First floors), the replies to the above Written Questions also noted that the Assembly Commission has implemented a wide range of accessibility initiatives. These included initiatives that were undertaken formally as part of agreed actions in successive Disability Action Plans, and more informal initiatives brought about through consultation with building users and visitors.These arrangements included disabled parking spaces; wheelchair access to the building and to the public areas in the Chamber and in Committee rooms; a braille tour guide; accreditation under the “Louder than Words” charter (until its withdrawal in February 2022 but the principles and practices are still applied); and the adoption of the Autism Access Award to enable a more autism-friendly experience in Parliament Buildings.Physical alternations to Parliament Buildings have included the installation of external and internal access ramps, alterations to staircases and lifts, the installation of the ‘Changing Places’ facility, the installation and upgrading of hearing loop systems and, naturally, the provision of automatic-opening doors in the Basement, Ground floor and First floor corridors. Continuous transmission of Assembly proceedings is now available via the headphones in the Chamber and public gallery during Assembly business.The Assembly Commission will continue to work closely and give due consideration to opportunities to improve the facilities in Parliament Buildings and will consult with Members and all other building users when any workable proposals are being developed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for her Department's assessment of how many homes across Northern Ireland do not have an adequate level of (i) cavity wall insulation; (ii) loft insulation; or (iii) both.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland House Condition Survey, carried out by the Housing Executive, presents a comprehensive overview of Northern Ireland’s dwelling stock and its occupants.  The most recent complete survey was undertaken in 2016, with a full report on the findings published in May 2018. https://www.nihe.gov.uk/Working-With-Us/Research/House-Condition-Survey . Sections 7.5 (Dwelling Insulation) and 7.6 (Loft Insulation) on pages 77-79 of the full report set out the numbers requested.  However, the House Condition Survey does not collect data on the adequacy of cavity wall insulation.The Housing Executive recently carried out a further Northern Ireland wide survey and the results of this work will be available January 2026.

answer_text: AQW 3717/22-27Andy Allen has askedThe Minister for Communities when she last engaged with the Minister for the Economy regarding the development of intervention, in response to rising energy costs.Answer		Officials continue to engage with Department for Economy, the Utility Regulator, and Consumer Council to identify potential options to provide support with increasing energy costs in the coming months.I am deeply frustrated that the continued absence of a functioning Executive and agreed Executive budget prevents additional measures being introduced.My Department will do all it can to support those experiencing hardship, and all options are being explored to provide both immediate and medium to longer term interventions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the cost of living policy recommendations her Department has received from the reconstituted Emergencies Leadership Group.
answer_text: I reconvened the VCS Emergencies Leadership Group on Cost of Living to help inform my Department’s immediate and medium to longer term policy responses. The role of the group is to focus on providing my Department with advice and representation, by identifying and defining the needs within communities, in response to the cost of living crisis, as well as highlighting emerging issues and informing the development of a package of support measures, however any new interventions will require a functioning Executive and agreed budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on work to roll out the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of motorists impacted by the MOT computer system failure on 31 August 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has had any engagement with the UK Government on the roll-out of the Energy Bills Support Scheme in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I met with the Chancellor of the Exchequer and relevant partners to discuss this matter on Wednesday 10 August, and again on Monday 15 August, where everyone agreed that we need a solution which was simple and ensured people in Northern Ireland saw the payment around the same time as elsewhere in the UK. I reminded the Chancellor that his predecessor made a commitment on the floor of the House of Commons to deliver the scheme to the whole of the UK. A solution was proposed by the Utility Regulator whereby Treasury would deliver the energy support scheme through direct payments to local energy providers, and customers in Northern Ireland would receive payments at the same time as people elsewhere in the UK. It is my firm view that any delay, brought about by unnecessary additional steps, is unacceptable, and I continue to push the Chancellor on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the steps her Department is taking to dismantle paramilitary structures.
answer_text: The ending of paramilitarism in our society is a task which requires a co-ordinated effort across society. The Member will be aware that the Executive Programme on Paramilitarism and Organised Crime is an ambitious, innovative cross-Departmental and multi-disciplinary initiative designed to address this complex problem and to create safer communities, resilient to paramilitarism, criminality and coercive control through a whole of government, public health approach. The Programme has had a positive impact in reducing the vulnerability of many individuals to paramilitary influence and in addressing the harmful effects of paramilitary violence. The Programme also includes the Paramilitary Crime Task Force, which is specifically tasked with dealing with the criminality of paramilitary groups and which has been successful in, among other things, taking drugs and firearms off our streets. For more information on the PSNI’s operations on the disruption and dismantling of paramilitary groups, I suggest you contact the Chief Constable.However, such activity will only successfully dismantle paramilitary structures when we have removed the conditions that allow those groups to exercise coercive control over communities and individuals. Therefore, collectively, we need a greater focus on the hidden harms that are key ways in which paramilitary groups maintain their position in our communities, such as gatekeeping, lowering aspirations for our communities, and restricting access to services. This requires political leadership and is something which every Minister and MLA has committed to through our pledges of office. We have committed to challenge all paramilitary activity and paramilitary attempts to control communities. We need to ensure that those behaviours are actively demonstrated by everyone working with and delivering services for those communities affected by paramilitarism and that we undermine the status of people or groups who seek to exercise coercive control.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the introduction of unexplained wealth orders.
answer_text: Asset recovery powers are key tools in the fight against serious organised crime.  Ensuring that law enforcement agencies in Northern Ireland have access to the full range of asset recovery powers in line with their counterparts in England, Scotland and Wales is a key priority.  In June 2020, I asked the Home Secretary to commence the outstanding proceeds of crime provisions of the Criminal Finances Act 2017, including unexplained wealth orders (UWOs) and accompanying interim freezing orders.  Following work by my Department and the Home Office to prepare commencement legislation and to publish statutory Codes of Practice providing guidance on the operational application of the new powers, the relevant provisions of the 2017 Act were brought into operation on 28 June 2021.  This enabled specified enforcement agencies to bring forward an application for a UWO to the High Court in Northern Ireland.  It is my understanding that the High Court in Northern Ireland has not made a UWO at this time, although UWOs may be under consideration in ongoing investigations.  The enforcement authorities with the powers to seek UWOs are operationally independent and I am unable to comment on specific operational matters. While there have been no UWOs to date, other asset recovery tools are used on an ongoing basis, including applications by the PSNI and the NCA for the new Account Freezing Orders and Listed Asset Orders introduced by the 2017 Act.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of (i) successful; and (ii) unsuccessful applications to the Affordable Warmth Scheme since its inception.
answer_text: From 1 September 2014 to 30 June 2022, 36,657 referrals have been received from local Councils to the Affordable Warmth Scheme. Of those referrals, 11,026 were ineligible or declined to proceed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 1086/22-27, for her Department's assessment of the fuel poverty rate in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: There is a global fuel crisis and it is having a major impact, particularly on those who are already struggling financially. I am deeply frustrated that the lack of a functioning Executive and agreed Executive budget prevents additional measures being introduced. However, I am committed to doing all I can to support to those who are experiencing real hardship.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any projected underspend on programmes within her Department.
answer_text: In the absence of a functioning Executive and agreed Executive budget, my Department is operating within a Contingency Budget Planning Envelope.  No underspends are currently forecast on programmes funded within this financial envelope.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has developed proposals to support businesses with rising energy costs.
answer_text: The unabated rises in energy bills, driven by our continued reliance on importing fossil fuels, highlights the importance of our local Energy Strategy ‘The Path to Net Zero Energy’.   One of the key objectives of the strategy is to ensure that all businesses have access to essential and affordable energy to enable an adequate standard of competitiveness. The focus is now on priorities for delivery to ensure that we maximise the benefits for our society and economy as soon as possible.In addition, Invest NI delivers a suite of support, including specialist advice and investment capital, to enable NI businesses to become more efficient and resilient through green efficiencies.  This includes fully funded Technical Consultancy, a Resource Matching Service, and the Resource Efficiency Capital Grant. This support helps businesses to optimise and futureproof their operations by implementing green efficiencies to achieve carbon savings and cost savings to mitigate the impact of rising energy costs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the amount spent on welfare mitigations in 2021-22; and (ii) the amount returned to the Department of Finance.
answer_text: My Department was allocated £42.8m for Welfare Mitigations in 2021-22.  Officials are currently working to produce the 2021-22 Annual Report and Accounts.  Final spend on Welfare Mitigations and any underspend will not be known until the accounts have been audited and finalised.

answer_text: The Welfare Mitigations independent advisory panel have presented their final report, including recommendations for new mitigation measures, to the Department. The report will be published at the earliest opportunity.The implementation of any new payment schemes will require Executive agreement on the allocation of necessary funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the current Universal Credit staffing levels; and (ii) the number of staff required to (a) maintain service delivery; and (b) manage the transition from the legacy system.
answer_text: Universal Credit currently has 2,354 staff delivering a professional and effective service to our customers. My Department is currently in the early stages of planning for the movement of customers from legacy benefits to Universal Credit. These plans will include ensuring that we have sufficient resourcing in place in advance of transition

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the steps her Department is taking to increase the number of social housing new builds.
answer_text: I have developed a Housing Supply Strategy which sets out a long term framework for collaborative action to increase supply, including how we can deliver homes of different types to meet a range of housing needs. I am currently considering options for progressing the Strategy in the continuing absence of an Executive and agreed budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what engagement his Department has had with colleagues in the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency, Swansea, regarding the consideration of adapting the road tax processes so those currently waiting for an MOT appointment are not excluded from obtaining road tax.
answer_text: The Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) work very closely with the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) on a range of common issues to try and ensure the best service possible is provided to the people here.  However, Vehicle Excise Duty (Motor Tax) is an excepted matter that is administered by the DVLA in Swansea on behalf of HM Treasury.  The DVA has no authority or influence with the DVLA on these matters.The DVA has, however, made provision for customers who require an MOT to tax their vehicle and cannot secure a date before their MOT expires.  Their advice is to:book the earliest available appointment; andkeep checking the booking system for an earlier appointment.If they get to within five days of the expiry date of their vehicle tax and they have been unable to secure an MOT appointment or their vehicle tax has expired, they should contact the DVA Customer Services team at dva.customerservices@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk, and they will do their best to get an urgent appointment for the vehicle.  To date, all customers who have contacted the DVA in these circumstances have been offered an earlier appointment slot.  The DVA has confirmed that any customer requiring an MOT to tax their vehicle will be given priority.The latest information on MOTs is available on NI Direct at https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/articles/book-view-change-or-cancel-mot-vehicle-test  and is also included as an advice note with every MOT Reminder Letter issued to their customers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the changes to the eligibility criteria for free school meals in each of the last 10 years.
answer_text: In 2010/11 the eligibility criteria for free school meals (FSM)  was extended to include full-time nursery and primary school children whose parents are in receipt of Working Tax Credit (WTC) and have an annual taxable income which does not exceed £16,190 (in 2010/11).  The new criterion was introduced on a phased basis with nursery, Foundation Stage and Key Stage 1 pupils eligible from September 2010. This was extended in September 2011 to Key Stage 2 pupils. It was further extended to post-primary school pupils from September 2014.  Similarly Uniform Grant eligibility criteria was amended in 2010/11 to allow for parents in receipt of WTC with the same threshold to apply for the uniform grant (UG).  This criterion did not extend to nursery school children. Foundation Stage and Key Stage 1 pupils were eligible from September 2010 and Key Stage 2 pupils became eligible from September 2011.    The current eligibility criteria for both FSM and UG were last updated in September 2017 solely to accommodate the introduction of the Universal Credit (UC) criterion. A consultation was carried out from April to June 2017. The Department’s primary aim was to ensure that those most in need are able to access free school meals and uniform grants following the introduction of UC.  The intention was that the new criterion should approximately maintain the numbers of pupils entitled within the available resources. More recently the Department has commenced a Review of Free School Meals and Uniform Grant eligibility criteria.  The review is considering a range of options including, but not limited to, maintaining the status quo, raising or removal of the earnings/income thresholds, universal FSM for certain year groups or all pupils; the broader DE arrangements for the provision of school meals to ensure they are up to date and the robustness of the current school uniform guidance and the rate of the UG. Consideration will also be given to putting uniform guidance on a legislative footing.  A costed options paper will be developed with public consultation planned for this Autumn.

Consideration will also be given to putting uniform guidance on a legislative footing.  A costed options paper will be developed with public consultation planned for this Autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the (i) scoring matrix; and (ii) assessment criteria, for awarding the youth intervention funding for 2022/23.
answer_text: The scoring matric and assessment criteria for awards in respect of youth intervention funding is an operational matter for Education Authority Youth Service (EAYS).As is the case with all Youth Funding, specific criteria for each funding stream is published on the EA Funding website (www.eanifunding.org.uk).  Details of the assessment criteria for 2022/23 youth intervention funding is found at Appendix 1 of the Application Guidance Notes – see Application Guidance tab at www.eanifunding.org.uk/plannedintervention/.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to provide a breakdown of all projects awarded youth intervention funding in 2022-23.
answer_text: The breakdown of funding in respect of youth intervention projects is an operational matter for the Education Authority Youth Service (EAYS).The EA Youth Funding website (www.eanifunding.org.uk) provides details of all youth funding, including Planned Intervention funding (www.eanifunding.org.uk/plannedintervention/ - see funded groups section). Details of youth intervention project awards for 2022/23 is not currently available pending confirmation of acceptance of proposed award by successful groups.  Once the necessary confirmations are completed, details will be published on the Planned Intervention webpage.  EAYS also provides a range of targeted interventions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the current position of the Living Over the Shops Scheme.
answer_text: An independent review of the scheme was carried out in 2016 and a copy of the report can be accessed online at the following link – 	https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/communities/dfc-pacec-lots-final-report-september-2016.pdf

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of people who reported problems using their High Street Scheme card, broken down by the reason.
answer_text: The Department does not hold this information nor is it possible to quantify exactly how many people had problems with their cards.  The Department is aware that some card holders were able to activate their cards but were unable to spend the full balance due to reasons associated with their card such as faulty chips. However, there were other card holders who were unable to spend the full balance on their cards due to human error such as entering an incorrect PIN on numerous occasions resulting in a blocked card or having a transaction declined due to insufficient funds.In recognition of the fact that some verified applicants did not receive a card while some others encountered difficulties activating and/or using their card, I announced that I would be making remedy payments to eligible applicants. The Department identified and contacted all those verified applicants who had a balance of £10 or more at Scheme closure who may be eligible for a remedy payment. To date 7,729 people have successfully applied for a remedy payment, with £624,724 being paid to successful applicants.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the total spend on delivering the High Street Scheme, broken down by spend category.
answer_text: Some costs relating to the scheme, such as remedy payments, are still ongoing and it is not possible at this moment in time to give a definitive figure in terms of overall costs. Some costs associated with the administration of the scheme are treated as commercially sensitive information and cannot be released until the contract period has ended.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the spending categories of the High Street Scheme.
answer_text: My Department published statistics on the HSS scheme on 13 April 2022. That publication includes a breakdown by the number of transactions and amounts spent by Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code and by Local Government District.		This analysis is available at the below link:	https://www.economy-ni.gov.uk/publications/analysis-northern-ireland-high-street-scheme

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether she will publish the most recent legal advice received on the rollout of the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.
answer_text: Legal Advice is privileged and not shared.  Therefore this advice will not be published.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps Health and Social Care Trusts are taking to support care in the community assistants with rising fuel costs.
answer_text: I recognise the difficulties faced by our healthcare staff due to rising fuel costs and I have approved a 50% increase to the secondary rate on annual mileage over 3,500 miles to bring it up to 30p per mile. This measure, which will be for an initial six month period, to be reviewed, will benefit those who are required to use their vehicles most frequently.Paying this increased rate through staff payroll systems will take some time to implement, in the interim, I have asked for further work to be undertaken to explore the feasibility of issuing fuel cards to eligible staff when they reach the 3,500 threshold.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the process required to deliver the one-off cost of living payments announced by the Chancellor in May 2022.
answer_text: Information on the Cost of Living payments can be found at https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/articles/cost-living-support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps Health and Social Care Trusts are taking to support staff, who are required to use their vehicles for work, with rising fuel costs.
answer_text: I recognise the difficulties faced by our healthcare staff due to rising fuel costs and I have approved a 50% increase to the secondary rate on annual mileage over 3,500 miles to bring it up to 30p per mile. This measure, which will be for an initial six month period, to be reviewed, will benefit those who are required to use their vehicles most frequently.Paying this increased rate through staff payroll systems will take some time to implement, in the interim, I have asked for further work to be undertaken to explore the feasibility of issuing fuel cards to eligible staff when they reach the 3,500 threshold.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount spent on the Casement Park project to date.
answer_text: To date, £11,335,043 of grant funding has been expended by the Department on the Casement Park Project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the primary legislation that was required to allow HMRC to make payments from the Energy Payment Support Scheme to those in receipt of tax credits.
answer_text: Policy and legislation responsibility for tax credits sits with Revenue and customs.  As taxation is an excepted matter, which sits outside the competency of the Assembly, my Department cannot advise about the primary legislation Revenue and Customs would require to make a payment to those in receipt of tax credits corresponding to that provided under the Energy Payment Support Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether she sought Executive approval to include people in receipt of tax credits as part of the Energy Support Payment Scheme.
answer_text: I refer the member to AQW 1684/22

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the future of Pine Lodge in East Belfast.
answer_text: The refurbishment of the Annex at Pine Lodge is now complete and has been occupied by the Encompass team since Feb 2022. The roll out of the encompass project is a regional priority and this building provides a hub for the Trust team.The Trust has received the feasibility study on the main Pine Lodge building and is reviewing the option to create an intermediate care facility for patients as they transition from acute hospital care. A significant capital investment will be required to progress this option. Given the pressure on acute hospital sites and the lack of available development locations within the Belfast area, Pine lodge remains a strategically important site for the Belfast Trust.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how much it would cost to include people in receipt of tax credits in the Energy Payment Support Scheme.
answer_text: I refer the member to AQW 1684/22

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities on what date she formally requested HMRC or the UK Government to make payments under the Energy Payment Support Scheme to those in receipt of tax credits.
answer_text: I refer the member to AQW 1684/22

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department is considering the introduction of a residential solar energy grant scheme.
answer_text: The Energy Strategy for Northern Ireland, published in December 2021, sets the policy direction for increasing renewable energy across Northern Ireland, and achieving a target of at least 70% of electricity consumed in Northern Ireland coming from a diverse range of renewable sources by 2030 (since raised to 80% by the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022). Essential to meeting this target will be establishing a support scheme(s) for renewable electricity in Northern Ireland and, under the Energy Strategy Action Plan for 2022, officials are currently focused on securing a scheme that will support large scale development that will offer maximum benefit to Northern Ireland as a whole while achieving the greatest value for money.  However, in line with the objectives of the Energy Strategy in putting the consumer at the heart of the energy transition and delivering a smart, decentralised energy system, we will also look at how to support other forms of generation, including micro-generating technologies such as residential solar photovoltaics, in a considered way as we bring forward future Action Plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether a formal request was made to the UK Government to table primary legislation that would enable payments under the Energy Payment Support Scheme to be made to people on tax credits.
answer_text: In my statement in the Assembly on 1 March 2022 I advised that officials engaged with Revenue and Customs (HMRC), who have responsibility for administering tax credits, to determine whether tax credits could be included within the eligibility criteria for the Energy Payment Support Scheme.  	HMRC advised that they did not have the legal powers to make a payment on behalf of the Department and to do so would require Primary Legislation in Westminster. It was therefore not possible for a workable solution to be found, that would enable the eligibility criteria to be extended to include tax credits, in the timescales available to deliver the Energy Payment Support Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what engagement she has had with local councils regarding making play parks accessible for children with disabilities.
answer_text: Responsibility for play parks lies with the local councils.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount spent from the High Street Scheme on (i) gas; (ii) electricity; (iii) coal; and (iv) oil.
answer_text: Transactions on the Spend Local Card are categorised in terms of the business/organisation at which the expenditure was made utilising Merchant Category Codes (MCC). Information on what people bought with their Spend Local card is not available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has considered establishing a cross-departmental working group to consider how departments can collectively reduce departmental energy costs.
answer_text: Government departments are facing significant energy cost increases that have been further exacerbated by budgetary pressures across the entire central government estate. The Energy Management Strategy and Action Plan to 2030 sets out a pathway for central government to establish effective energy management processes that unlock value and include an objective of lowering net energy consumption by 30% by 2030 from a 2016/17 baseline year. This strategy was approved by the NICS board in January 2019 and formally launched in June 2019. My department leads an Energy Management Unit in partnership with the Department of Finance and the Strategic Investment Board to manage the delivery of the Energy Management Strategy and Action Plan to 2030 for central government.  Delivering this strategy and action plan is a collective responsibility across central government and the role of the unit is to facilitate Departments and Arm’s Length Bodies to deliver on potential energy-savings opportunities to manage the risk that rising and volatile energy costs pose for government.Over the last three years, and in alignment with the strategy Action Plan, an extensive energy consumption data collection process has been undertaken to ensure a robust, validated and verified dataset is in place allowing for more accurate and strategic decision making to improve energy management in the central government estate. This process has been further supported by the inception of a centrally procured framework delivering a service to provide automatic meter reading systems. In alignment with the strategy Action Plan, the Energy Management Unit has undertaken an extensive period of communication across central government through regular energy manager’s forums and bilateral discussions with Centres of Procurement Excellence. The discussions have not only considered collaborative energy procurement and buying strategies but have considered other key enablers such as piloting behavioural change and building on a recently commissioned exemplar buildings study. Collaboration and engagement across central government to drive awareness and influence decision making has been the emphasis of discussions to date. 	It is vital that in government we move to deliver on our vision, as set out in the NI Executive’s Energy Strategy, of self-sufficiency in affordable renewable energy.  This will break the current link with volatile global fossil fuel markets, and lower energy bills for all consumers in Northern Ireland, including Departmental energy costs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the Access and Inclusion Programme budget for 2022-23.
answer_text: In the absence of a functioning Executive and agreed budget, my Department continues to deliver capital programmes through contingency arrangements.

answer_text: Since its inception in 2018/19, my Department has supported 230 projects under the Access and Inclusion Programme with a total value of £4,704,578. A breakdown of the projects has been placed in the Assembly Library due to the volume of the information it contains.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 30342/17-22, to detail the total number of recommendations.
answer_text: Details of proposals cannot be shared in advance of consideration by a functioning Executive Committee.

answer_text: I have received legal advice in respect to the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer and this confirms that I have a responsibility to bring my proposals to the Executive for the necessary approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the total amount paid out by CAPITA in goodwill gesture payments following personal independence payment complaints.
answer_text: Since the introduction of the Personal Independence Payment Assessment Services Contract in June 2016 Capita has made goodwill payments totalling £11,125.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 840/22-27, to detail the steps being taken to reduce processing time.
answer_text: A range of short term contingency measures were taken which helped manage the temporary increased in application demand caused by the Omicron surge from December 2021 – March 2022.  Long term permanent measures are being developed to manage what is now expected to be a sustained increase in demand for all Discretionary Support provisions due to the rising costs of living.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many staff involved in processing discretionary support applications have been absent in each of the last 12 months.
answer_text: Finance Support staff cover a range of duties across Discretionary Support and Social Fund including application processing, telephony, payments, accuracy and reviews.  As many staff deploy across those disciplines as needed, figures are reported across the Finance Support team.The average monthly unplanned absence level, e.g. sick absence, within Finance Support in the last 12 months was just under 7%, equating to approximately 18 staff.		Planned absence, such as annual leave, training, etc., is accounted for in the staff levels allocated to each branch.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of vacant staff positions within discretionary support and the duration of the vacancies.
answer_text: There are currently no vacant staff positions in Discretionary Support. Recruitment plans are in place for further additional staff to meet an anticipated rise in demand throughout the remainder of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 230/22-27, whether he will consider the creation of such a grant.
answer_text: There are no plans to create a grant for the installation of residential septic tanks.   Advice or guidance on regulatory controls and compliance for domestic septic tank installation can be obtained through NIEA at nieaconsents@daera-ni.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of meetings held with (i) officials; and (ii) stakeholders since July 2021, in relation to the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.
answer_text: I have regular engagement with my officials in relation to the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer. I have met with representatives from the Irish FA, NI Football League and clubs including Coleraine FC and Limavady United FC.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the resource required to maintain grass cutting across Northern Ireland, against the available budget.
answer_text: The budget for grass cutting cannot be confirmed until an overall budget for 2022-23 has been agreed.  However, my Department has commenced a programme of grass cutting that is similar to last year with two cuts being carried out across the road network, along with some additional cutting at sightlines for road safety purposes.Grass cutting is undertaken by the Department’s internal contractor and external contractors, with the percentage split undertaken by each varying across Council areas. In all areas, resources are currently in place to deliver the initial cut and the position will be reviewed when the budget is confirmed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what steps her Department is taking to provide further support to the charity and social enterprise sector.
answer_text: AnswerThe Department is responsible for charity regulation, and support for charities is shared across all departments in line with relevant policy remits.DfC provides funding support for running and project costs to a wide range of not for profit organisations, many of which are charities, in the areas for which DfC has policy responsibility, including the arts, heritage, sports, languages, museums and voluntary and community sectors. The Department also provides funding for infrastructure support through NICVA and others which support charities with advice on good governance, regulation, funding and Covid recovery actions.The Department for Economy has policy responsibility for social enterprises.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline the steps her Department is taking to increase energy efficiency in residential properties, broken down by sector.
answer_text: My Department offers a range of support to improve energy efficiency.	The Affordable Warmth Scheme is available to low-income owner-occupiers and households in the private rented sector with an income of under £23,000 per annum and includes multiple measures such as loft and cavity wall insulation, installation of gas or oil, boiler replacement, replacement windows and solid wall insulation.The Boiler Replacement Scheme provides a grant of up to £1,000 towards the cost of replacing old and inefficient boilers over 15 years old. This is open to owner-occupiers with a household income of less than £40,000 per annum.In relation to social homes, my Department, on behalf of the Housing Executive, was successful in securing €22.951 million funding from the European Regional Development Fund (ERDF) for their Energy Efficiency in Social Housing Programme with match funding from the Housing Executive. The programme aims to improve the thermal efficiency of non-traditional construction homes within the Housing Executive stock by December 2023. The works to improve cladding, new double glazing and insulation will give tenants warmer homes, resulting in more comfortable and healthier living conditions. These works will also help meet the 2050 net zero carbon target.My Department is also committed to increasing the energy efficiency of new build homes and is supporting sustainable and energy efficient design beyond the statutory minimum by allowing Housing Associations to claim a supplementary 6% ‘Energy Efficiency Multiplier’ for new social dwellings where their dwellings are evidenced to achieve the following technical requirements:1. SAP Band ‘A’ (92-100)2. A minimum Fabric Energy Efficiency Standard of:- 39 kWh/m2/year for apartment blocks and mid-terrace homes- 46 kWh/m2/year for end terrace, semi-detached and detached homesMy work to improve energy efficiency is also reflected in The Private Tenancies Act. The Act, which received Royal Assent in May 2022, gives my Department the power to bring forward regulations which will stipulate the minimum Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) a privately rented property must achieve before it can be let. 	I have developed a Housing Supply Strategy which acknowledges the importance of ensuring we have good quality homes. Options for the Strategy are being considered in the absence of a functioning Executive and agreed budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any departmental underspend in the 2021/22 financial year and the reason for the underspend.
answer_text: My Department is currently working to produce the 2021-22 Annual Report and Accounts. Any Departmental underspend will not be known until these have been audited and finalised.
